revision.text:
'''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）- [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若いころから刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守とともに[[宿禰]]姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に[[連座]]して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[大和国|大和守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において称徳天皇の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は大和国造正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『続日本紀』（上・中・下巻）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまと の ながおか}} [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:689年生]] [[Category:769年没]]

'''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）- [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若いころから刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守とともに[[宿禰]]姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に[[連座]]して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[大和国|大和守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において称徳天皇の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は大和国造正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『続日本紀』（上・中・下巻）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまと の ながおか}} [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:大和守]] [[Category:駿河守]] [[Category:689年生]] [[Category:769年没]]

'''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）- [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若いころから刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守とともに[[宿禰]]姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に[[連座]]して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[大和国|大和守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は大和国造正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『続日本紀』（上・中・下巻）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまと の ながおか}} [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:遣唐使]] [[Category:689年生]] [[Category:769年没]]

[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[大和国|大和守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は大和国造正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。

revision.text:
'''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか、[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）- [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若いころから刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守とともに[[宿禰]]姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に[[連座]]して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[民部省|民部大輔]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[河内国司|河内守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]81。最終[[官位]]は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]][[連座]]） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（大和国造正四位下）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年

== 経歴 == 若いころから刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守とともに[[宿禰]]姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に[[連座]]して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[民部省|民部大輔]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[河内国司|河内守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も[[作法]]を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に[[年齢]]を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]81。最終[[官位]]は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）[[施行]]された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]とともにその名が挙がっている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜[[あしぎぬ|絁]]20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]][[連座]]） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（大和国造正四位下）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大和長岡 | 画像 = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大倭宿禰長岡 菊池容斎『前賢故実』より | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = [[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]） | 死没 = [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]） | 改名 = 小東人（初名）→長岡 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]] | 主君 = [[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇]]→[[淳仁天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]] | 氏族 = 大倭[[忌寸]]→[[宿禰]]（[[倭国造]]） | 父母 = 父：[[大倭五百足]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法道|明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若い頃から刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守と共に宿禰姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に連座して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[民部省|民部大輔]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[河内国司|河内守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も作法を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に年齢を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
神護景雲3年（769年）制定され、[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）施行された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]と共にその名が挙がっている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜絁20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]]連座） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：卒去（大和国造正四位下）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまと の なかおか}} [[Category:倭氏|なかおか]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:遣唐使]] [[Category:689年生]] [[Category:769年没]]

revision.comment: 加筆・修正。

== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜絁20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]]連座） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：卒去（大和国造正四位下）

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大和 長岡 | 画像 = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大倭宿禰長岡/[[菊池容斎]]『[[前賢故実]]』より | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = [[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]） | 死没 = [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]） | 改名 = 小東人（初名）→長岡 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]] | 主君 = [[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇]]→[[淳仁天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]] | 氏族 = 大倭[[忌寸]]→[[宿禰]]（[[倭国造]]） | 父母 = 父：[[大倭五百足]]？ | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法道|明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[刑部卿|刑部少輔]]・[[大倭五百足]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。
== 経歴 == 若い頃から刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守と共に宿禰姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に連座して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[民部省|民部大輔]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[河内国司|河内守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も作法を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に年齢を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
神護景雲3年（769年）制定され、[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）施行された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]と共にその名が挙がっている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜絁20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]]連座） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9年（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：卒去（大和国造正四位下）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年

== 経歴 == 若い頃から刑名の学を好み、[[紀伝道|文章]]作成を得意とした<ref name="a">『[[続日本紀]]』神護景雲3年10月29日条</ref>。[[霊亀]]2年（[[716年]]）[[遣唐使]]に請益生として随行し入[[唐]]する。それまで疑問とされていた事項について在唐中に多く解明したとされ、帰国後は[[明法道|法律]]を論じる者は長岡に付き従い教えを請うたという。[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]）『[[養老令]]』撰定の功労により[[功田]]4町を与えられた（この時の位階は[[従六位|従六位上]]）。
[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守と共に宿禰姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に連座して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。
[[孝謙天皇|孝謙]]朝から[[淳仁天皇|淳仁]]朝にかけて、[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）従五位上、天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]、天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]）正五位上、[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]）[[従四位|従四位下]]と順調に昇進し、[[駿河国|駿河守]]・[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]・[[民部省|民部大輔]]・[[京職|左京大夫]]・[[河内国司|河内守]]を務める。しかし、地方官としては政治に仁恵がなく、部下の官人や人民はこれを患えたという<ref name="a" />。天平宝字8年（[[764年]]）右京大夫に任ぜられるが、老齢のため致仕。
[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も作法を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に年齢を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
神護景雲3年（769年）制定され、[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）施行された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]と共にその名が挙がっている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従七位|従七位上]] *[[養老]]6年（[[722年]]） 2月27日：[[功田]]4町 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） 11月22日：[[忌寸]]から[[宿禰]]に[[改姓]]、[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、賜絁20疋 *天平10年（[[738年]]） 閏7月7日：[[刑部省|刑部少輔]] *天平13年（[[741年]]） 正月22日：[[流罪]]（[[藤原広嗣の乱]]連座） *天平16年（[[744年]]） 9月15日：[[西海道]][[巡察使]]次官 *天平18年（[[746年]]） 4月：[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]] *天平19年（[[747年]]） 正月20日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（[[内位]]） *天平21年（[[749年]]） 2月27日：摂津亮 *[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） 正月16日：従五位上 *天平勝宝3年（[[751年]]） 正月25日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *天平勝宝5年（[[753年]]） 4月22日：[[駿河国|駿河守]] *天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。6月16日：[[紫微中台|紫微大忠]]。日付不詳：小東人から長岡に改名 *時期不詳：[[民部省|民部大輔]] *天平宝字3年（[[759年]]） 5月17日：[[京職|左京大夫]] *天平宝字4年（[[760年]]） 日付不詳：[[河内国司|河内守]] *[[天平宝字]]7年（[[763年]]） 正月9日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *天平宝字8年（[[764年]]） 日付不詳：右京大夫 *[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]） 正月1日：[[正四位|正四位下]]（越階） *神護景雲3年（[[769年]]） 10月29日：卒去（大和国造正四位下）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1992年 *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、1995年
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまと の なかおか}} [[Category:倭氏|なかおか]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:遣唐使]] [[Category:正四位下受位者]] [[Category:689年生]] [[Category:769年没]]

revision.comment: [[巡察使]]⇒[[巡察使 (古代日本)]] に内部リンクさしかえ。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大和 長岡 | 画像 = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大倭宿禰長岡/[[菊池容斎]]『[[前賢故実]]』より | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = [[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]） | 死没 = [[神護景雲]]3年[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]（[[769年]][[12月1日]]） | 改名 = 小東人（初名）→長岡 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]] | 主君 = [[元正天皇]]→[[聖武天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇]]→[[淳仁天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]] | 氏族 = 大倭[[忌寸]]→[[宿禰]]（[[倭国造]]） | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''大和 長岡'''（やまと の ながおか）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]・[[明法道|明法家]]。初名は'''小東人'''。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[忌寸]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[大和国|大和]][[国造]]。

[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）神の宣託があったとして、一族の水守と共に宿禰姓を賜与され、他の同族は[[連]]姓に[[改姓]]した。また同時に[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙の上、[[絁]]20疋を与えされた。天平10年（[[738年]]）[[刑部省|刑部少輔]]に任ぜられる。天平13年（[[741年]]）[[藤原広嗣の乱]]に連座して[[流罪]]となるが、のち赦されたらしく、天平16年（[[744年]]）[[西海道]][[巡察使 (古代日本)|巡察使]]次官、天平18年（[[746年]]）[[摂津国#摂津職|摂津亮]]を経て、天平19年（[[747年]]）には[[従五位|従五位下]]に叙せられている。

[[神護景雲]]2年（[[768年]]）賀正の宴において[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の[[詔]]により特に[[昇殿]]を許されるが、鬢髪は豊かで所作も作法を違わなかったことから、[[天皇]]に年齢を問われ、80歳になったと答えると、天皇は久しく感嘆して自ら位記を書き[[正四位|正四位下]]に昇叙した。翌神護景雲3年（[[769年]]）10月29日卒去。享年81。最終官位は[[倭国造|大和国造]]正四位下。
神護景雲3年（769年）制定され、[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）施行された[[刪定律令]]24条の撰者の一人として、[[吉備真備]]と共にその名が挙がっている。

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | po..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1992 * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 2)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 1992 * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 3)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 1995

By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), [[Category:New He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. {{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 9: Promoted to [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Yamato no Nagaoka]][[Category:Search started pages]]January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. {{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[Empress Kōken]] → [[Emperor Junnin]] → [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[Nara period]] [[noble]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[Kentōshi]] mission to [[Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[Gyōbu-shō|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[Empress Kōken]] and [[Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
