He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433599]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] → [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[:en:Rank|Rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. During the reigns of [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433599]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] → [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His [[:en:Rank|Rank]] was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] for his contributions to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]]. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]], later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]], he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]]. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of [[:en:kuden|kuden]] * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of [[:en:aya|aya]] cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed [[:en:Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|Gyōbu Shōbu]] * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to [[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō Inspector]] * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433599]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} → {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His {{ill|Rank|en|Rank|WD=Q4120621|lt=Rank}} was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden}} for his contributions to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya}} cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu}}. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector}}, later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}, he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami}}. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden}} * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya}} cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu}} * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector}} * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami}} * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433599]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Sukune [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} → {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His {{ill|Rank|en|Rank|WD=Q4120621|lt=Rank}} was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden}} for his contributions to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya}} cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu}}. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector}}, later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}, he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami}}. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden}} * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya}} cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu}} * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector}} * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami}} * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)'', {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}} Academic Library, 1992 * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 2)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 1992 * Ujitani, M. ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 3)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 1995

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大和長岡]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} → {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His {{ill|Rank|en|Rank|WD=Q4120621|lt=Rank|1=Rank}} was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden|1=Kuden}} for his contributions to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector|1=Saikaidō}}, later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}, he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami|1=Kawachi Province}}. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden|1=Kuden}} * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}} * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector|1=Saikaidō}} * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami|1=Kawachi Province}} * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),
January 9: Promoted to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), Date unknown: Appointed [[Ukyo Daibu]] * [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768),
January 1: Promoted to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]] (Special Promotion) * [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769), October 29: Passed away (Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko, Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)

Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector|1=Saikaidō}}, later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}, he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami|1=Kawachi Province}}. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden|1=Kuden}} * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}} * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
January 22: Exiled for involvement in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] * [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), September 15: Assistant to {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector|1=Saikaidō}} * [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), April: Appointed [[Settsu no Suke]] * [[Tenpyō 19]] (747),
January 20: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750),
January 16: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751),
January 25: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 5]] (753), April 22: Appointed [[Suruga no Kami]] * [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), May 20: Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]], June 16: Appointed [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], renamed from Kotohito to Nagaoka * Date unknown: Appointed [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 3]] (759), May 17: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]] * [[Tenpyō Hōji 4]] (760), Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami|1=Kawachi Province}} * [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763),

{{Infobox person|image=File:Yamato no Sukune.jpg|birth_date=0689|death_date={{death date|0769|12|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Yamato no Nagaoka | image = Yamato no Sukune.jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Yamato no Nagaoka, from [[Yosai Kikuchi]]'s ''[[Preeminent Ancient Deeds]]'' | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | birth = 3rd year of [[Empress Jitō]] (689) | death = 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769) | birth_name = Kotohito (initial name) → Nagaoka | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]; {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] | allegiance = [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Emperor Shōmu]] → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} → {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} | clan = Yamato [[Imiki]] → [[Sukune]] (descendant of [[Kuni no miyatsuko of Yamato]]) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }} '''Yamato no Nagaoka''' (やまと の ながおか) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and [[Myōbōdō|legal scholar]]. His initial name was '''Kotohito''' (小東人). He bore the [[kabane]] [[Imiki]] but later changed to [[Sukune]]. His {{ill|Rank|en|Rank|WD=Q4120621|lt=Rank|1=Rank}} was [[Senior Fourth Rank|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]], and he served as [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
== Career == From a young age, Nagaoka had a passion for the study of penal law and was skilled in [[Kidentō|literary composition]]<ref name="a">''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', entry for the 3rd year of [[Shingo Keiun]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]]</ref>. In [[Reiki 2]] (716), he accompanied the [[:en:Kentōshi|Kentōshi]] mission to [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]] as a student. During his time in Tang, he clarified many previously unresolved issues. Upon his return, Nagaoka became a leading authority on [[Myōbōdō|law]], with students seeking his teachings. In [[Yōrō 6]] (722), he was awarded four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden|1=Kuden}} for his contributions to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}''. At the time, he held the Rank of [[Junior Sixth Rank, Upper]].
In [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), following a divine oracle, he and his relative Mizumori were granted the [[kabane]] Sukune, while other clan members were renamed to [[Muraji]]. Concurrently, Nagaoka was promoted from [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer) and received 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth. In [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), he was appointed as {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}. Despite being implicated in the [[:en:Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion]] in [[Tenpyō 13]] (741) and exiled, he was later pardoned. By [[Tenpyō 16]] (744), he served as assistant to the {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō Inspector|1=Saikaidō}}, later becoming [[Settsu no Suke]] in [[Tenpyō 18]] (746), and was promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Inner) in [[Tenpyō 19]] (747).
During the reigns of {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}, he steadily advanced: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 2]] (750), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 3]] (751), [[Senior Fifth Rank, Upper]] in [[Tenpyō Shōhō 9]] (757), and [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower]] in [[Tenpyō Hōji 7]] (763). He held various positions, including [[Suruga no Kami]], [[Shibi Chūtai|Shibi Daichū]], [[Minbushō|Minbu Daisuke]], [[Kyōshiki|Sakyō Daibu]], and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi no Kami|1=Kawachi Province}}. However, he was criticized for his lack of benevolence in governance, leading to suffering among his subordinates and the populace<ref name="a" />. In [[Tenpyō Hōji 8]] (764), he became [[Ukyo Daibu]], but soon retired due to old age.
In [[Shingo Keiun 2]] (768), during a New Year’s banquet, he was granted special permission to attend court. Empress [[Kōken|Shōtoku]] was impressed by his vitality and rich hair at the age of 80. When asked his age, he replied, prompting the empress to write his promotion certificate herself, raising him to [[Senior Fourth Rank, Lower]]. Nagaoka passed away on [[Shingo Keiun 3]], [[October 29 (lunar calendar)|October 29]] (December 1, 769), at 81. His final Rank was [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Yamato Kuni no miyatsuko]], Senior Fourth Rank, Lower.
He was one of the authors of the ''[[Santei Ritsuryō]]'', established in [[Shingo Keiun 3]] (769) and implemented in [[Enryaku 10]] (791), alongside [[Kibi no Makibi]].
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper]] * [[Yōrō 6]] (722), February 27: Granted four chō of {{ill|kuden|en|kuden|WD=Q629640|lt=kuden|1=Kuden}} * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper]] * [[Tenpyō 9]] (737), November 22: Changed from [[Imiki]] to [[Sukune]], promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower]] (Outer), awarded 20 bolts of {{ill|aya|en|aya|WD=Q2819612|lt=aya|1=Aya}} cloth * [[Tenpyō 10]] (738), Intercalary July 7: Appointed {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Gyōbu Shōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}} * [[Tenpyō 13]] (741),
